sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Staleness detection tells you which functions have code that changed without a corresponding documentation update. It's the core quality signal in cortex.

sections.heading: Quick Start
sections.content:
```bash
# Check for stale docs (problems only)
cortex check .

# Full inventory including CLEAN nodes
cortex check . --all

# CI gate: fail if any stale nodes
cortex check . --fail-on stale

# Machine-readable output
cortex check . --format json
```

Output: `3 CODE_STALE · 1 COVERAGE_STALE · 1 STRUCTURAL_DRIFT · 241 CLEAN`

sections.heading: Core Functionality
sections.content:
- **CODE_STALE:** Code body changed but the doc hasn't followed. Resolve by updating the docstring or verifying it's still accurate via `cortex mark-clean`.
- **DESC_STALE:** Doc was updated without the code changing — prose may have drifted speculatively. Review whether the doc still reflects what the code actually does.
- **CONTENT_STALE:** (Doc nodes only) A linked code node changed after the section was written. Must update the prose — cannot be verified away.
- **COVERAGE_STALE:** (Test nodes only) The code this test validates changed. Review the test to confirm it still exercises the right behavior. Cannot be verified away.
- **STRUCTURAL_DRIFT:** Function was renamed or deleted. The node no longer maps to source code. Rebuild to discover the new name or remove the node.
- **VERIFIED:** Was stale but a human or agent confirmed the docs are correct. Expires automatically if code changes again.
- **CLEAN:** Code and doc are in sync. Not explicitly verified but no known drift.

sections.heading: Configuration & Options
sections.content:
| Option | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `--fail-on none` | Never exit 1 | Default |
| `--fail-on stale` | Exit 1 if CODE_STALE or STRUCTURAL_DRIFT | — |
| `--fail-on any` | Exit 1 if any non-CLEAN/non-VERIFIED nodes | — |
| `--all` | Include CLEAN nodes in output | Problems only |
| `--format json` | JSON output: `{statuses: {…}, summary: {…}}` | Text |

sections.heading: Best Practices & Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### Best Practices
- Run `cortex check .` before committing to catch stale docs early
- Add `cortex check . --fail-on stale` to CI pre-merge checks
- Use `cortex mark-clean` for cosmetic code changes where docs are still accurate

### Common Issues
- **Problem:** Everything shows CLEAN after first build but you expect stale nodes
- **Solution:** Expected on baseline. Make a code change and rebuild — CODE_STALE will appear on next `cortex check`.

- **Problem:** CODE_STALE after reformatting code (no logic change)
- **Solution:** `cortex mark-clean <node_id> . --reason 'cosmetic reformat only'`

- **Problem:** DESC_STALE on a node but the code looks right
- **Solution:** The doc was updated more recently than the code. Review whether the prose still matches the implementation.
